questions.q: What is the main objective of the Consumer Protection Act, 1986?
questions.answer: The main objective of the Consumer Protection Act, 1986 is to provide better protection to consumers and for that purpose to make provision for the establishment of consumer councils and other authorities for the timely and effective disposal of consumers' grievances and for matters connected therewith.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The _______________ is a three-tier quasi-judicial machinery at the national, state and district levels to redress consumer grievances.
What are the rights of consumers as per the Consumer Protection Act, 1986? Explain.
questions.answer: The rights of consumers as per the Consumer Protection Act, 1986 are: (i) right to safety, (ii) right to be informed, (iii) right to choose, (iv) right to be heard, (v) right to seek redressal and (vi) right to consumer education.

questions.q: State whether the statement is True or False: The District Consumer Protection Council is established at the state level.
What is the role of the Central Consumer Protection Council?
questions.answer: The Central Consumer Protection Council is established to promote and protect the rights of consumers. It is a advisory body which meets at regular intervals to discuss the issues related to consumer protection and makes recommendations to the government.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a right of consumers? (A) Right to safety (B) Right to be informed (C) Right to employment (D) Right to choose
Explain the concept of 'consumer' as defined under the Consumer Protection Act, 1986.
questions.answer: As per the Consumer Protection Act, 1986, a consumer is a person who buys or hires any goods or services for a consideration which has been paid or promised or partly paid and partly promised, or under any system of deferred payment, and includes any user of such goods or services other than the person who buys or hires such goods or services, when such use is made with the approval of the person who buys or hires such goods or services.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The Consumer Protection Act, 1986 provides for a _______________ -tier quasi-judicial machinery to redress consumer grievances.
What are the functions of the District Consumer Protection Council?
questions.answer: The District Consumer Protection Council is established to promote and protect the rights of consumers in the district. Its functions include: (i) to review the provision and implementation of the laws which are affecting the consumers, (ii) to encourage the establishment of voluntary consumer organizations and (iii) to render advice to the government on consumer welfare.

questions.q: State whether the statement is True or False: The National Consumer Protection Council is established at the district level.
